pom.xml.general_idea: Update Maven compiler plugin configuration to use the 'release' flag for Java 21 instead of separate source/target flags.
pom.xml.why_exists: The 'source' and 'target' flags are deprecated in newer compiler plugin versions and do not enforce API compatibility with the target JDK; using 'release' is required for correct cross-compilation behavior in modern toolchains.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade the maven-compiler-plugin version to a snapshot compatible with Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of the maven-compiler-plugin may not support compiling code for Java 21 or may lack necessary bug fixes for newer JDKs.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update Maven properties to set the compiler source and target to Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: The project properties must reflect the new target JDK version to ensure consistency across the build lifecycle.
